title: Linociera angolensis
taxonomy: Linociera angolensis is classified within the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapote family. This family is a part of the order Ebenales, which is a group of flowering plants that have a distinct set of characteristics. The order Ebenales is a part of the clade Pentapetalae, which is a larger group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the pentapetalous angiosperms. The clade Pentapetalae is part of the superclade Rosidae, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. These classifications are based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships.
uses: Linociera angolensis has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of Africa, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and bark are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
